_status: HELD-INACTIVE 2026-07-13: primary gauge 07189000 is dead (cfs-only, offline since 2026-04-27; 00065 returns -999999). Access points/services/prose/geometry done. BLOCKER: recalibrate to live Noel gauge 07188925 (datum 5.8-11 ft, thresholds do not transfer) with an OBSERVED floatable ladder before reactivating. See verified-identifiers-elk.md 2026-07-13 note.
name: Elk River
description: A warm, popular summer float in the far-southwest Missouri corner (McDonald County) around Pineville and Noel. Formed at Pineville where Big Sugar Creek and Little Sugar Creek meet; Indian Creek roughly doubles the flow near mile 30. Gravel bars, bluffs, and heavy summer party-float traffic. The base flow is clear and spring-influenced, but the river is strongly rain-driven and flash-flood prone — flows can swing from a couple hundred cfs to many thousands within hours of a storm — so it runs warmer and flashier than the deep-Ozark spring rivers.
managingAuthority: Missouri Department of Conservation (state-managed accesses); streambed navigability per Missouri law

accessLaw.value: Missouri follows the "navigable in fact" doctrine: the public may float and wade streams navigable in fact, but streambeds/banks are frequently privately owned, so access is legal from public road crossings, public land, and established accesses, while portaging or camping on private gravel bars can be trespass. Navigability is decided stream by stream; there is no statewide list. (Shared Missouri paragraph — confirm the AGO/MDC citation once for the state.)
accessLaw.source: https://mdc.mo.gov/ (MDC stream access) — confirm AGO citation once for MO

gauges.name: Elk River near Tiff City, MO
gauges.knownBias: near the OK line below the Indian Creek confluence; over-reads the popular upper Pineville–Noel reach

sections.name: Elk River (Pineville → Noel → Tiff City)
sections.description: The full Elk River summer-float run from Pineville down through Noel to the Tiff City / Oklahoma-line area — roughly 20–23 floatable miles, the standard 1- to 2-day trip the Noel and Pineville outfitters shuttle. Formed at Pineville by the junction of Big Sugar Creek and Little Sugar Creek; Indian Creek enters near mile 30 and roughly doubles the flow, so the lower reach runs bigger than the upper Pineville stretch. Clear, spring-influenced base flow over gravel bars and past bluffs, but strongly rain-driven and flash-flood prone. Hazards include the low-head dam at Noel (Shadow Lake, ~mile 35.8, mandatory portage) and a concrete low-water bridge near mile 30.7.

takeOut.name: Cowskin Access (MDC, Hwy 43, near the Tiff City gauge)

representativeGauge.rationale: Elk River near Tiff City (07189000) is the only real-time USGS gauge on the Elk mainstem (00060 discharge + 00065 gage height), sitting near the downstream Tiff City end of the reach. Thresholds are quoted against it in gage-height FEET per the Elk River Floats Gauge Height Key; the current USGS rating disagrees with the historical observed cfs, so the badge keys on feet, not cfs.
representativeGauge.knownBias: gauge is near the OK line (~mile 45) BELOW the Indian Creek confluence (Indian Creek enters ~mile 30 and roughly doubles the flow), so it OVER-READS the popular upper Pineville–Noel reach; an upper put-in runs lower than the gauge shows

thresholds.source: Elk River Floats Gauge Height Key: below 2.5 ft the lower trip runs canoes/kayaks only (scraping); the upper & full 12-mi trips are closed below 3.5 ft.
corroboratingSources: Elk River Floats Gauge Height Key (https://www.elkriverfloats.com/ — per-trip gauge-height table)

thresholds.source: Elk River Floats Gauge Height Key: at 3.5–5 ft all trips run all craft (canoes, kayaks, rafts, tubes) — the comfortable all-craft range.
Elk River Floats Gauge Height Key: above 5 ft tubes and canoes drop out and craft are restricted to experienced kayaks/rafts.
Elk River Floats Gauge Height Key: at 6+ ft the upper & full (12-mi) trips close ('no watercraft — lower trip only') — do not float the main river. Deliberately NOT the NWS flood stage (15 ft).
corroboratingSources: Elk River Floats Gauge Height Key (https://www.elkriverfloats.com/ — per-trip gauge-height table)

publishedFloatTimes.reachLabel: Elk River Floats 'full' trip (upper + lower combined)
publishedFloatTimes.source: Elk River Floats (elkriverfloats.com) trip structure referenced by the Gauge Height Key, which rates the 'upper', 'full', and 'lower' trips by gage height; the full trip is ~12 river miles. Float hours were not published in the findings.

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: below ~3.5 ft rafts/tubes drag and the full trip closes
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: rises fast after rain; flash-flood prone; Noel/Shadow Lake dam portage

hazards.value: Flash-flood prone: clear spring-influenced base flow but the river rises extremely fast after rain (Big Sugar Creek headwaters swing from ~20 cfs to ~20,000 cfs), so a floatable morning can turn dangerous within hours.
hazards.source: elk-findings-partial.md hydrology note (rain_flashy classification; Big Sugar swings ~20 → 20,000 cfs)

hazards.value: Low-head dam at Noel (Shadow Lake), ~river mile 35.8 — MANDATORY PORTAGE. Low-head dams create dangerous recirculating hydraulics; do not run.
hazards.source: elk-findings-partial.md access/hazard notes: 'Noel/Shadow Lake (low-head DAM ~mi35.8 — PORTAGE)'

hazards.value: Concrete low-water bridge ~river mile 30.7 — strainer / pin hazard, especially at elevated flows.

localKnowledge: Heavy summer party-float scene: the Pineville → Noel → Tiff City run is one of southwest Missouri's busiest warm-weather float rivers, with big weekend and holiday crowds, tubes, and coolers. At least seven outfitter/campground liveries operate on the reach (Elk River Floats, Kozy Kamp, Eagles Nest, Big Elk Floats & Camping, River Ranch Resort, Shady Beach Campground, Two Sons Floats & Camping), most clustered around Noel and Pineville, offering shuttles, camping, and cabins.

Warmer and flashier than the deep-Ozark spring rivers; primarily a summer-season float. Formed at Pineville by Big Sugar Creek + Little Sugar Creek; Indian Creek joins near mile 30 and roughly doubles the flow, so the lower reach below the Tiff City gauge runs bigger than the upper Pineville stretch.

accessPoints.name: City of Pineville Access
accessPoints.notes: Uppermost put-in for the reach, at Pineville where Big Sugar Creek and Little Sugar Creek join to form the Elk River. River mile not pinned in the findings (upstream of the US-71 bridge at ~mi24.5). Coordinates PENDING human placement [manual] — findings coords approximate and omitted.

accessPoints.name: US-71 Bridge Access
accessPoints.notes: US-71 highway bridge crossing, ~river mile 24.5, near the top of the float reach. River mile approximate; coordinates PENDING human placement [manual].

accessPoints.name: Mount Shira Access (MDC)
accessPoints.notes: MDC river access, ~river mile 32, above Noel. River mile approximate; coordinates PENDING human placement [manual].

accessPoints.name: Noel / Shadow Lake Access
accessPoints.notes: HAZARD: low-head dam (Shadow Lake) at ~river mile 35.8 — MANDATORY PORTAGE. Popular Noel outfitter hub. River mile approximate; coordinates PENDING human placement [manual].

accessPoints.name: Sycamore Landing
accessPoints.notes: Take-out landing on the lower reach between Noel and the Tiff City gauge. River mile not pinned in the findings; coordinates PENDING human placement [manual].

accessPoints.name: Cowskin Access (MDC)
accessPoints.notes: MDC access on Highway 43, ~river mile 45, at/near the Elk River near Tiff City gauge (07189000) — the downstream take-out for the reach. River mile approximate; coordinates PENDING human placement [manual].

USGS GNIS feature 01092538 (Elk River); NHD / National Map HUC 11070208
openQuestions: CALIBRATION UPDATED 2026-07-12 from the Elk River Floats gauge-height key (owner-provided): added the optimal band (3.5–5 ft all-craft) and set dangerous=6 ft (upper/full trips close). This RESOLVES the earlier 'no optimal → badge reads low everywhere' blocker. moherp confirms no observed cfs Good/High for the Elk mainstem (only an observed Low ~250 cfs), so the ft outfitter key is the calibration source.

LAUNCH BLOCKER — OPTIMAL WINDOW UNPROVEN: there is NO moherp OBSERVED 'Good' rating anywhere on the Elk mainstem — only a single stale 2010 'Low' point (3.56 ft / ~238 cfs) — so optimal_min/optimal_max are OMITTED and the site's model-ESTIMATED 'Good' (~482 cfs) is REJECTED (same estimated-tier miscalibration seen on the Black). CONSEQUENCE: with no optimal band the condition badge reads 'low' across the ENTIRE floatable range, from the 3.5 ft too_low edge all the way up to the 6 ft high anchor. Elk needs an OBSERVED optimal calibration (a trip-rated 'Good' gage height, or Elk River Floats 'ideal-level' guidance) before it can badge accurately — this is the main launch blocker.

HYDROLOGY (RESOLVED): reclassified spring_fed_float -> rain_flashy. Clear, spring-influenced base flow but strongly flash-flood prone — Big Sugar Creek headwaters swing from ~20 cfs to ~20,000 cfs. Thresholds are therefore encoded in FEET of gage height (matching the outfitter Gauge Height Key), NOT converted to cfs, because the current USGS stage-discharge rating disagrees with the historical observed cfs.

REPRESENTATIVE-GAUGE BIAS: Elk River near Tiff City 07189000 sits near the OK line (~mile 45) BELOW the Indian Creek confluence (Indian Creek enters ~mile 30 and roughly doubles the flow), so it OVER-READS the popular upper Pineville–Noel reach. Quantify the over-read so upper-reach put-ins can be corrected.

ACCESS GPS PENDING: every access point (City of Pineville, US-71 bridge ~mi24.5, MDC Mount Shira ~mi32, Noel/Shadow Lake dam ~mi35.8, Sycamore Landing, MDC Cowskin ~mi45) has APPROXIMATE river miles and NO verified coordinates — a human must place every point in admin [manual].

BIG SUGAR CREEK drainage area UNKNOWN: tributary gauge Big Sugar Creek near Powell 07188653 is deliberately NOT in gauges[] (feeder, not mainstem); its drainage area was not pinned in the findings, so no drainage-area transfer to the mainstem is possible yet.
INDIAN CREEK GAUGE ID CONFLICT: candidate ids conflict — 07188885 (near Lanagan) vs 07188870 (at Anderson); neither verified. Resolve before adding Indian Creek as a secondary/context gauge.

NHD IDENTIFIER: the Elk has NO single NHD Permanent_Identifier (~100 flowline segments; representative mainstem PIDs 86154849 / 86155179 / 86154487). GNIS 01092538 (HUC 11070208) is available as the stable app identifier; nhdFeatureId left 'UNKNOWN' pending a chosen representative reach PID.

TIFM7 NWS flood stages (action 13 / minor flood 15 ft) are a CROSS-CHECK ONLY — the dangerous anchor is the recreational outfitter 'no watercraft' level (6.5 ft gage height), deliberately NOT the NWS flood stage.
